message:
perf(scheduler): skip cancel-lane rebuild when re-promotion priority is same or lower

When cancel_promote() is called for a task already in the cancel lane,
the previous code unconditionally rebuilt the entire BinaryHeap via
drain().map().collect() even when the new priority was equal to or lower
than the existing one — a no-op that wasted O(n) work.

Now the code extracts the existing priority first via find().map(), and
returns early when priority <= existing_priority. The heap rebuild only
runs when the priority actually increases, which is the uncommon case.

Also simplified the rebuild closure: since we already proved the new
priority is strictly higher, the inner comparison is unnecessary — we
can unconditionally apply the new priority and generation to the matched
task entry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
